# Team News: Turkey vs Paraguay injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 19 Jun 2026 04:00

© Imago / Yulian Todorov

Group D rivals Turkey and Paraguay will continue their World Cup 2026 campaign on Friday, after both lost their opening fixture.

Already lagging behind the USA and Australia, maximum points are a must in San Francisco, where the losing side could be set for an early exit.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news.

---

## TURKEY vs. PARAGUAY

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/turkey/world-cup-2026/team-news/GAME%20PAGE%20URL%20HERE

## TURKEY

Out: None

Doubtful: Kenan Yildiz(calf)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Cakir; Celik, Demiral, Bardakci, Kadioglu; Calhanoglu, Yuksek; Guler, Kokcu, Yilmaz; Akturkoglu

## PARAGUAY

Out: None

Doubtful: Gustavo Caballero (muscular), Ramon Sosa (ankle)

# Turkey vs Paraguay Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group D

Two teams that opened with painful defeats meet in a game that already feels decisive. Turkey were stunned 2-0 by Australia, a result that shocked a side many fancied to advance, and Paraguay were thumped 4-1 by the USA. Both sit on zero points in a Group D where USA and Australia have pulled clear, and the loser here is almost certainly heading home.

Turkey are slight favorites at home in Santa Clara, and they have the more talented squad, with Arda Guler, Ke

# Turkey vs Paraguay: Team News, Predicted Lineups and Match Pre...

Author: Dicip Khetan
Published: 2026-06-19T22:34:00+08:00
Source: ibtimes.sg (ibtimes.sg)
Language: en

## Story

Turkey vs Paraguay: Team News, Predicted Lineups and Match Pre...

# Turkey vs Paraguay: Team News, Predicted Lineups and Match Prediction

By Dicip Khetan

Türkiye duo Uzun and Yildiz FIFA.com

- Turkiye lost 2-0 to Australia in its opening Group D match
- Paraguay suffered a 4-1 defeat to the United States
- Arda Guler and Hakan Calhanoglu remain central to Turkiye's hopes
- Defeat would significantly damage either side's knockout-stage chances

Turkiye and Paraguay will battle to keep their FIFA World Cup 2026 hopes alive when they meet in a pivotal Group D clash at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara on Saturday.

Both nations enter the contest without a point after disappointing starts to the tournament. Turkiye suffered a 2-0 defeat to Australia, while Paraguay were beaten 4-1 by the United States, leaving both teams needing a result to remain firmly in the race for a place in the knockout rounds.

With the United States and Australia currently setting the pace in the group, defeat coul

news_items.title: Paraguay XI vs Turkiye – Predicted lineup and team news - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: The Football Faithful Fri, June 19, 2026 at 7:10 AM UTC · 2 min read
news_items.text:
Paraguay XI vs Turkiye – Predicted lineup and team news - Yahoo Sports

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

TRENDING

World Cup: Day 8 recap

U.S. Open Round 1 leaderboard

Knicks championship parade recap

World Cup panic meter

Story by

Paraguay XI vs Turkiye – Predicted lineup and team news

Paraguay arrive at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara desperate to rebuild after a chastening opening defeat.

Gustavo Alfaro watched his side’s defensive structure crumble against the United States on matchday one. Three goals conceded before half-time in Los Angeles left La Albirroja with a mountain to climb, and they never recovered, eventually falling to a 4-1 defeat.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Their reputation for defensive resilience took a serious dent, and Alfaro will demand an immediate and emphatic response tonight. A win against Turkiye would keep their knockout hopes alive; anything less and their tournament is effectively over.

## Paraguay team news

Alfaro faces a couple of fitness concerns ahead of kick-off. Gustavo Caballero missed the opener entirely and continues to battle a muscular issue, while Ramon Sosa requires a late assessment

Paraguay XI vs Turkiye – Predicted lineup and team news

Paraguay arrive at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara desperate to rebuild after a chastening opening defeat.

Gustavo Alfaro watched his side’s defensive structure crumble against the United States on matchday one. Three goals conceded before half-time in Los Angeles left La Albirroja with a mountain to climb, and they never recovered, eventually falling to a 4-1 defeat.

Advertisement

Their reputation for defensive resilience took a serious dent, and Alfaro will demand an immediate and emphatic response tonight. A win against Turkiye would keep their knockout hopes alive; anything less and their tournament is effectively over.
